description: The Sonora Cartel was a Mexican drug trafficking organization founded in 1987 by Miguel Caro Quintero and Rafael Caro Quintero, operating primarily in northern Mexico and the southwestern United States. It was considered one of the oldest and best-established cartels by the DEA, with direct links to Colombian cartels. The cartel was acquired by the Sinaloa Cartel by 2007 and reported defunct by 2010.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Sonora Cartel is believed to be one of the earliest cartels to begin shipping cocaine from Colombia , particularly from the Cali Cartel .
country_links.context: The Sonora Cartel sourced cocaine from Colombia, particularly from the Cali Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Sonora cartel had direct links to Colombian drug cartels and operated routes into California , Arizona , Texas , and Nevada .
country_links.context: The Sonora Cartel operated drug smuggling routes into California, Arizona, Texas, and Nevada.
